title: acalypha communis subsp. communis
intro: Acalypha communis subsp. communis, also known as the common cup plant or red calypha, is a species of flowering plant in the spurge family, characterized by its distinctive red or purple flower spikes and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Acalypha communis subsp. communis include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as acting as a natural insect repellent. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
